frames.name: Regional Diplomatic Engagement
frames.description: Frames the story as a diplomatic summit where Caribbean leaders are seeking to reset relations with the U.S. amid policy concerns
frames.emphasis: The Caribbean Community summit as a venue for addressing U.S.-Caribbean relations and the arrival of Secretary Rubio for dialogue

frames.name: Humanitarian Crisis Concern
frames.description: Emphasizes the humanitarian impact of U.S. policies, particularly the oil embargo's effects on Cuba's basic services
frames.emphasis: Energy rationing, suspended medical surgeries, halted flights, and broader regional destabilization fears

frames.name: U.S. Security Doctrine Implementation
frames.description: Presents the Donroe Doctrine as a comprehensive security strategy involving military action and economic pressure
frames.emphasis: Military strikes on drug trafficking, the Maduro capture operation, and broader hemispheric security objectives

frames.name: Mixed Regional Response
frames.description: Highlights the divided Caribbean response to U.S. actions, with some praise and significant concerns
frames.emphasis: Some leaders praising military actions (particularly Maduro's capture) while many express concern about humanitarian and economic consequences

outlierPerspectives: The Treasury Department's apparent policy softening on Cuban oil restrictions, mentioned only briefly as new guidance allowing Venezuelan oil resales for humanitarian purposes
The specific framing of this as a 'decisive hour' for the Caribbean region amid shifting global order and uncertain supply chains
missingContext: Specific details about which Caribbean leaders support vs. oppose U.S. actions
The full scope and legal framework of the Donroe Doctrine beyond the mentioned actions
Economic data on the actual impact of the oil embargo on Cuba and regional trade
Details about the Treasury Department's new guidance and its practical implications
Historical context comparing the Donroe Doctrine to previous U.S. Caribbean policies
Perspectives from Cuban officials or other affected parties beyond Caribbean leaders
Specific outcomes or agreements from the Caribbean Community summit discussions
